summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is the definition of 't-test for slope'?

"t-test for slope" means: Tests H₀: β₁ = 0 using t = b₁/SE(b₁) with df = n - 2; determines whether x is a significant linear predictor of y. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Null hypothesis for slope'?

"Null hypothesis for slope" means: H₀: β₁ = 0 states that there is no linear relationship between x and y in the population. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Connection to correlation'?

"Connection to correlation" means: Testing β₁ = 0 is equivalent to testing ρ = 0; both assess whether a linear relationship exists. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'ANOVA F-test'?

"ANOVA F-test" means: In simple regression, F = t²; the F-test from the ANOVA table produces the same p-value as the t-test for the slope. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Practical significance'?

"Practical significance" means: A statistically significant slope may have little practical importance if the effect is very small; always consider R² and the magnitude of b₁. The other options describe different concepts.
